revenue: $26.46 billion in Q2 2025 (Total revenue, net of interest expense)
net_income: $7.12 billion in Q2 2025 (Net income applicable to common shareholders was $6.83 billion)
key_metrics: Diluted earnings per share: $0.89 (up from $0.83 in Q2 2024)
Return on average tangible common shareholders' equity (ROTCE): 13.40%
qoq_changes: Total revenue decreased sequentially to $26.46 billion from $27.37 billion in Q1 2025
Net income declined sequentially to $7.12 billion from $7.40 billion in Q1 2025
Provision for credit losses increased sequentially to $1.59 billion from $1.48 billion in Q1 2025
new_risks: Sequential increase in provision for credit losses ($1.59B vs $1.48B in Q1) suggests potential credit quality normalization or deterioration
Quarter-over-quarter contraction in both top-line revenue and net income indicates potential macroeconomic or interest rate headwinds
management_tone: Neutral, as the bank demonstrated solid YoY EPS growth and maintained a strong 13.40% ROTCE, but faced sequential top- and bottom-line contraction alongside rising credit provisions.
analyst_note: Bank of America posted a mixed Q2 2025, delivering $0.89 in diluted EPS to beat the prior year's $0.83, though sequential performance softened with revenue dropping to $26.46 billion. The uptick in credit loss provisions to $1.59 billion warrants close monitoring, but the bank's core profitability remains resilient with an efficiency ratio of 64.93%.
